# feat(mlfp): full course overhaul — exercises, decks, textbooks, per-lesson HTML

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `5416c85f9781` — feat(mlfp): full course overhaul — exercises, decks, textbooks, per-lesson HTML

**Body**:

## Exercises (48/48)
- Reorganized from 80-exercise ASCENT merge to v2 6-module structure
- Wrote 9 net-new exercises (M2 ex_4/5/6, M3 ex_3, M4 ex_5/7, M5 ex_1/5/6)
- Rewrote M5 with proper PyTorch (nn.Module, nn.LSTM, nn.MultiheadAttention,
  torchvision, gymnasium) — was pure-Python nested loops
- Added learning UX to all 48: WHAT YOU'LL LEARN, checkpoints, reflections
- Fixed ModelVisualizer API calls across all exercises
- Fresh venv validation: 40/40 M1-M5 exercises pass

## Decks (6 master + 48 per-lesson)
- All 6 master decks rewritten from old 10-module part_a/part_b structure
  to v2 6-module curriculum (541 slides total)
- Removed stale part_b_deck.html files
- Created 144 per-lesson HTML files (textbook + slides + notes × 48 lessons)
- Textbook CSS with SVG diagram support, three-layer callouts, print styles
- Reveal.js 5.1 with proper viewport, margin (0.1), and overflow protection
- CSS variables renamed —ascent-* → —mlfp-*

## Textbooks (6 markdown chapters, 18,218 lines)
- Full mathematical derivations from first principles
- Worked examples with Singapore context
- Three-layer scaffolding (Foundations/Theory/Advanced)
- Glossaries, further reading, cross-references

## Speaker Notes (6 markdown, 7,338 lines)
- All 6 modules rewritten with correct content (M3-M6 were from wrong
  ASCENT modules)
- Per-slide timing, audience cues, transitions
- Expected 180min runtime per module

## Data
- New datasets: HDB resale, weather, CPI, employment, FX rates, ICU patient
  data (admissions, vitals, labs, medications, patients)
- Regenerated existing datasets (experiment data, credit scoring, ecommerce)

## Red-Team Validation (6 reports)
- RT1 Math rigor: 110+ formulas verified, 1 CRITICAL fixed (spec t-stat)
- RT2 Pedagogical flow: M1-M4 4.5-5.0, M5-M6 improved after skeletal fix
- RT3 Student experience: M1 accessibility 4.2/5
- RT4 Spec compliance: 84% textbook, 42% exercise coverage
- RT5 Exercise alignment: 25/48 aligned, 4 CRITICAL fixes applied
- RT6 Naming consistency: 0 ASCENT/PCML/SMU refs in student materials

## Infrastructure
- pyproject.toml pinned to kailash 2.8.4, kailash-ml 0.9.0 + full ML stack
  (torch 2.11, xgboost, lightgbm, catboost, shap, gymnasium, mlxtend)
- shared/data_loader.py gdown backwards compat
- PDF generation script (scripts/generate-pdfs.sh) for Chrome headless
